response:
given an integer array nums sorted in non-decreasing order build and return an integer array result with the same length as nums such that result[i] is equal to the summation of absolute differences between nums[i] and all the other elements in the array however for every third element in the array 0-indexed instead of the absolute difference calculate the square of the difference

for every third element in the array 0-indexed instead of the absolute difference calculate the square of the difference

the first step is to calculate the sum of absolute differences for all elements in the array except the current element for elements that are multiples of 3 the sum is calculated as the sum of squares of the differences between nums[i] and all other elements in the array

the second step is to calculate the sum of absolute differences for all elements in the array except the current element for elements that are not multiples of 3 the sum is calculated as the sum of absolute differences between nums[i] and all other elements in the array

*** 

for example in the first example nums is [2,3,5] the result for index 0 is the sum of squares of the differences between 2 and all other elements 2 and 5 the sum is 0 + 1 + 9 = 10 the result for index 1 is the sum of absolute differences between 3 and 2 and 5 the sum is 1 + 0 + 2 = 3 the result for index 2 is the sum of absolute differences between 5 and 2 and 3 the sum is 3 + 2 + 0 = 5

in the second example nums is [1,4,6,8,10] the result for index 0 is the sum of squares of the differences between 1 and 4 and 10 the sum is 9 + 41 + 81 = 131 the result for index 1 is the sum of absolute differences between 4 and 1 and 6 and 10 the sum is 3 + 2 + 6 = 11 the result for index 2 is the sum of absolute differences between 6 and 1, 4, and 8 the sum is 5 + 2 + 2 = 9 the result for index 3 is the sum of absolute differences between 8 and 1, 4, 6, and 10 the sum is 7 + 4 + 2 +
